rulings.Case Title: RMHNS,INC. VS. SIM & YOON CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Aof Motion And Motion To Amend Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 20, 2009, line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANT SIM & YOON CORPORATION Motion To Amend Cross Complaint. Motion is denied. Motion is untimely and does not comply with CRC 3.1324. Counsel's declaration does not explain what recently occurred to warrant a cause of action for personal liability, or why this motion was not brought earlier. If the settlement transcript is erroneous, it is unclear why counsel did not raise that issue at the prior hearing. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CAL-MURPHY, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. HINES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Order Awarding Attorney Fees And Costs To X-Deft'S Murphy'S Deli Franchsing, Inc. And George Omran And Jeanette Omran Trustees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 20, 2009, line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT MURPHY'S DELI FRANCHISING INC., GEORGE OMRAN, and, JEANETTE OMRAN Motion For Order Awarding Attorney Fees and Costs to Cross-Defendants Murphy's Deli Franchsing, Inc. and George Omran and Jeanette Omran Trustees. Cross defendants' motion is granted in part. In view of the duplicative nature of the pleadings and excessive time spent researching and preparing the pleadings, the court finds reasonable and necessary time spent, including prosecuting this motion to be 100 hours. The court accepts counsel's billing rate of $425 per hour. The lodestar amount is $42,500 which the court finds to be reasonable attorney fees considering the pleadings, complexity of the issues, and the evidence presented. The court awards $3,045.75 in costs. Total attorneys fees and costs is $45,545.75. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE G. AGUILA et al VS. WALBRIDGE TERRACES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 20, 2009, line 8, PLAINTIFF CHRISTINE AGUILA, and, DARYL AGUILA Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Motion for preliminary injunction granted, as modified. Preliminary injunction granted that requires defendant to keep one unit vacant for plaintiffs at 1828-1888 Geneva Avenue meeting description of "future housing" in the Full Release and Settlement Agreement. As requested, the proposed injunction was "mandatory." A preliminary mandatory injunction is rarely granted and is subject to stricker review on appeal. The granting of a mandatory injunction is not permitted except in extreme cases where a right there to is clearly established. Teachers Ins. & Annuity Assn. vs. Furlotti (1999) 70 Cal.App.4th 1487. Plaintiffs are likely to prevail on the merits and demonstrate irreparable harm if the unit is rented or otherwise becomes unavailable. Plaintiffs have an inadequate remedy because the unit is unique. The cuort waives the bond requirement due to plaintiffs' indigency. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. ORLANDO GIL-REYES et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 20, 2009, line 11, PLAINTIFF JOHN STEWART COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. No opposition having been filed, the motion for summary judgment is granted. No triable issue of fact raised. Defendant breached the lease, was properly noticed and served, and remains in possession of the property. Plaintiff entitled to damages of rental amount of Fifteen dollars per day from April 28, 2009. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON BUTTERWORTH VS. LAURA ESMERALDA CONTRERAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 20, 2009, line 13, DEFENDANT LAURA CONTRERAS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment denied. Res judicata does not apply. Each case has been prosecuted under a different theory and no case has been adjudicated on the merits. Plaintiff is not judicially estopped from alleging a landlord-tenant relationship since he has accepted rent. Rulings on objections not necessary in view of the issues presented. =(302/CWW)
